title: linaria oblongifolia subsp. benitoi
intro: Linaria oblongifolia subsp. benitoi, also known as the Blue-eyed Mary, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Linaria oblongifolia subsp. benitoi include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
